summary: The apparent cross-variant zero-success blocker was caused by the harness hard-coding the wrong PROGRAM_ID bytes, which made every instruction fail with Anchor DeclaredProgramIdMismatch.

variant_snapshot.no_yield: after fix: 6452 executions, edges 630/8142, branches 561/4071, ok 17805/40417
variant_snapshot.crank: after fix: 3985 executions, edges 1163/9650, branches 1070/4825, ok 12713/25354
variant_snapshot.scaled_ui: after fix: 7718 executions, edges 637/9326, branches 568/4663, ok 19911/37902

likely_causes: the byte array behind PROGRAM_ID did not match the declared on-chain program id string
Anchor rejected every call before account validation, masking real product execution
resolution: Fix the PROGRAM_ID byte array to match 3C865D264L4NkAm78zfnDzQJJvXuU3fMjRUvRxyPi5da, then rerun each variant.
promotion_gate: This was a harness bug, not a KAST product defect. Use the post-fix runs for further discovery and adjudication.
